read-history-with-log-and-show.label: Read the History You Built
read-history-with-log-and-show.summary: Inspect the community-project history and committed file content without changing state.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.title: Read the Four-Commit Timeline
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.prompt:
Read the commit messages from top to bottom and confirm that they appear in this order:

1. `Update community contacts`
2. `Update community schedule`
3. `Add community contacts`
4. `Start community project`

Together, these messages tell the story of how the project developed without requiring you to open each file.

help.concept: `git log --oneline` lists commits newest first. It lets you audit the sequence of focused snapshots without changing repository state.
help.hint_2: Check the messages rather than memorizing commit identifiers.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.instructions: Read the concise history. Confirm that the newest entries are the contact update, the schedule update, the added contact file, and the original project snapshot.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Start community project'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Add community contacts'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM

mkdir -p -- community-project

Shared community activities and updates.

mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0130
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A

' > community-project/README.md

printf %s 'Community contacts

mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you read the four-entry timeline?

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.title: Inspect the Latest Snapshot Summary
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.prompt:
Use `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` to inspect that commit. The output combines its commit message with a compact summary of the file changed in the snapshot.

Confirm that the newest commit is `Update community contacts` and that its statistics include only `contacts.txt`.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.hint: Ask Git to show `HEAD` with a one-line subject and file statistics.

help.concept: `HEAD` names the current commit. `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` displays its subject plus a compact summary of the files changed in that snapshot.
help.hint_2: This inspection must not change status.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.instructions: Inspect the newest commit and its compact file statistics.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.title: Read a File from the Latest Snapshot
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.prompt: Read the committed version of `contacts.txt` directly from `HEAD` without opening or changing the working file.

help.concept: `git show HEAD:contacts.txt` prints that file exactly as stored in the current commit. It reads history and leaves the working tree untouched.
help.hint_2: Do not redirect the output into a file.

try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
try_read_history_with_log_and_show_sketc.instructions: Read the committed version of `contacts.txt` directly from `HEAD` without opening or changing the working file.

Did you read the committed contact file from `HEAD`?

single-choice-1.title: HEAD in Git
single-choice-1.prompt: What does `HEAD` identify in this course?
single-choice-1.hint: `HEAD` points to the commit currently checked out, not to the staging area or the first commit.

help.concept: Scan the repository timeline, open one checkpoint, understand `HEAD`, and move between overview and detail.

sketch0.title: Read the Project Timeline
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: The repository now contains four commits. Use the concise history command to view the project timeline with the newest commit first.

sketch1.title: Inspect the Current Snapshot
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: `HEAD` refers to the commit currently checked out—at this point, the newest commit in the project’s history.

sketch2.title: Read a File from History
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
The command `git show HEAD:contacts.txt` reads `contacts.txt` exactly as it is stored in the current commit.

The `commit:path` form lets you inspect a file from Git history without opening, modifying, or replacing the working copy. It also leaves the staging area unchanged.
